components.recommended_action: Rewrite using Jira Cloud REST API v3 and ScriptRunner Cloud Groovy
components.report_text: Script uses ComponentAccessor to fetch the IssueManager and UserManager, both of which are DC-only Java APIs. It also reads a custom field value by name using CustomFieldManager, then reassigns the issue to a user looked up by username. All four APIs are unavailable in Cloud.

components.recommended_action: Translate JSU validators to ScriptRunner Cloud Groovy validators
components.report_text: Component uses two JSU validators: FieldsRequiredValidator ensures 'Approver' and 'Business Justification' fields are populated before the Approve transition, and UserInGroupValidator checks the current user is in the 'change-managers' group. JSU is not available on Cloud; these must be rewritten as ScriptRunner Cloud validators.

components.recommended_action: Rewrite as a Python webhook receiver using Jira Cloud webhook registration via REST API
components.report_text: DC listener fires on IssueCreatedEvent and POSTs a custom payload to an internal Slack-compatible endpoint. Uses BasicAuth with a service account. In Cloud, this should be a registered webhook via /rest/api/3/webhook that sends to an external receiver; the receiver is rewritten in Python.
components.original_script:
import com.atlassian.jira.event.issue.IssueEvent
import com.atlassian.jira.event.type.EventType
import groovyx.net.http.HTTPBuilder
import static groovyx.net.http.Method.POST
import static groovyx.net.http.ContentType.JSON

def issue = event.issue
if (event.eventTypeId == EventType.ISSUE_CREATED_ID) {
    def http = new HTTPBuilder('https://hooks.internal.example.com')
    http.auth.basic('svc-jira', 'secret123')
    http.request(POST, JSON) { req ->
        uri.path = '/jira-events'
        body = [
            event: 'issue_created',
            key: issue.key,
            summary: issue.summary,
            reporter: issue.reporterUser?.name,
            project: issue.projectObject.key
        ]
    }
}

components.recommended_action: Translate DC automation rule to Jira Cloud Automation JSON format
components.report_text: Native DC automation rule: when an issue is created in the OPS project with Priority = Critical, assign it to user 'oncall-engineer' and send an email notification. DC rule uses legacy XML-based trigger syntax and username-based assignment which do not exist in Cloud Automation.
components.original_script:
<!-- DC Automation Rule (XML) -->
<rule name='Auto-assign Critical Incidents'>
  <trigger type='issue_created'>
    <project>OPS</project>
  </trigger>
  <condition type='field_value'>
    <field>priority</field>
    <value>Critical</value>
  </condition>
  <action type='assign_issue'>
    <username>oncall-engineer</username>
  </action>
  <action type='send_email'>
    <to>oncall@example.com</to>
    <subject>Critical incident created: {{issue.key}}</subject>
    <body>A critical incident {{issue.key}} was created. Please respond immediately.</body>
  </action>
</rule>
